scope_contract: cleanliness-of-THIS-diff only; new-in-this-diff verbosity violations per 'rules not stories'. Pre-existing historical verbosity is ignored (Section 5.4 rule 3).

findings.recommendation: ADVISORY ONLY (non-blocking). This is a docs/reference discoverability note, OUTSIDE the command/agent execution-prompt target class to which the 'rules not stories' verbose-section thresholds primarily apply. Its one narrative section ('Why this matters', lines 41-51, ~10 lines) is rationale prose ('operational driver of harness lies patterns') that leans story-over-rule, but a short rationale is appropriate and useful in a reference doc, and 10 lines is far below the 50-line minor threshold. No reduction required; if trimming is desired later, collapse the 'Why this matters' prose to a single sentence cross-referencing agents/qa.md Phase 5 step 6 and scripts/qa-manifest-guard.py. Does NOT force CLOSE: NO.

cleared.rationale: The 4 new Phase-5 items (steps 6-9, lines 1083-1092) are all dense normative enforcement rules using MUST/MUST NOT, exit-code bindings, and forbidden-shape definitions. They are execution rules, not stories. No verbose-section pattern was introduced (no ## Philosophy / ## Overview / ## Quality Standards / ## Safety Features / ## Usage). Net added content is ~11 lines in a 1906-line file (~0.6%), well below all severity thresholds. Item 9's inline 'because the failure is an infrastructure block' is rule-tied rationale (justifies the exit-3 -> primary_cause=environment branch), not detachable narrative — acceptable under 'rules not stories'. The _doc docblock expansion and schema field changes are functional contract surface, not prose. The 2 pre-existing-line tweaks (lines 96, 100) are wording substitutions, not new verbose content.

summary.close_recommendation: CLOSE: YES (no blocking verbosity introduced by this diff). One advisory non-blocking observation on docs/reference/venv-repair.md.
